destinations.evidence: LLM pricing errors propagate out of calculate_async_fees instead of falling back to HTTP executor pricing.
Settlement preparation returns an error when LLM fee calculation fails.
destinations.snippet:
let fees = calculate_async_fees(db, get_model_registry_address(), contract_job.precompile_address, &metadata.precompile_input, Some(execution_result), model_cache)
    .map_err(|err| format!("async fee calculation failed: {err}"))?;

destinations.evidence: The payload builder skips the async entry when settlement preparation fails, so it is not included with fallback HTTP pricing.

equivalence_argument: The failure path is split across shared fee calculation, settlement preparation, and payload candidate collection: compute_llm_executor_fee errors propagate as Err, prepare_fulfilled_async_settlement returns Err, and payload construction skips the fulfilled entry rather than substituting HTTP byte pricing.
notes: Mapped to calculate_async_fees error propagation and payload-builder skip behavior.
rationale: create_settlement_transaction now returns Result, maps compute_llm_executor_fee errors into an error instead of warning and using http_executor_fee, and the payload builder logs the failure and continues without including the async transaction. The commit body explicitly calls out a strict no-fallback policy. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: If LLM executor fee calculation fails, settlement creation must return an error and the payload builder must skip that async transaction instead of falling back to HTTP byte-based pricing.
suggested_falsification_tests: Feed the payload builder an LLM async transaction whose execution_result lacks required pricing metadata, and assert no settlement transaction is created and the async transaction is skipped rather than included with HTTP executor fees.
summary: Unpriceable LLM async results are skipped rather than priced with HTTP fallback fees.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Feed a fulfilled LLM async entry whose output lacks required pricing metadata and assert prepare_fulfilled_async_settlement returns an error and the payload builder skips the entry instead of creating an HTTP-priced settlement.
title: Unpriceable LLMs are not fallback-priced
